% Table~6: Related-work positioning for learned / LA eviction (author-maintained; not generated by build_kbs_main_manuscript_artifacts.py).

% natbib-compatible \citep{...} ; add refs/related_work_table6.bib to your bibliography input (or merge keys).
% Width: wrap in \begin{minipage}{\linewidth} ... \end{minipage} if embedding inside a column.
\begin{minipage}{\linewidth}
\centering
\footnotesize
\setlength{\tabcolsep}{3pt}
\begin{tabular}{@{}>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{0.17\linewidth}>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{0.24\linewidth}>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{0.14\linewidth}>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{0.16\linewidth}>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{0.25\linewidth}@{}}
\toprule
\textbf{Representative line} &
\textbf{Supervision / prediction target} &
\textbf{Decision object} &
\textbf{Robustness / integration} &
\textbf{Contrast to \texttt{evict\_value\_v1}} \\
\midrule
LA paging with ML advice \citep{lykouris2018competitive,bansal2022weightedpaging} &
Competitive online caching under ML advice; advice augments classical paging &
Eviction / paging under advice &
Competitive-ratio guarantees vs.\ advice error &
Theoretical LA paging results; we focus on an empirical supervised eviction scorer with trace-replay evaluation, not these algorithms themselves. \\
\addlinespace[0.15em]
Robust LA + untrusted predictions \citep{antoniadis2020online,wei2020lacaching,chledowski2021robustlacaching} &
Predictors (e.g., cache states or reuse signals) integrated via robust controllers or combiners &
Eviction under LA robust policies &
Trust/doubt, shadow experts, robust experimental LA caching &
Strong \emph{baselines} in our evaluation; not the same learning target as regressing on counterfactual finite-horizon miss counts. \\
\addlinespace[0.15em]
PARROT \citep{liu2020parrot} &
Imitation-style learning from strong / oracle eviction signals &
Learned replacement policy &
Learning pipeline around expert supervision &
Emphasizes imitation of strong eviction behavior; we supervise a scalar loss on \emph{counterfactual} caches instead of imitating a single expert action label. \\
\addlinespace[0.15em]
HALP \citep{song2023halp} &
Heuristic-aided learned \emph{preference} / eviction policy learning &
Production-scale eviction decisions &
Heuristics + ML co-design &
Close systems neighbor; distinction should be stated at the \emph{label} level (preference learning vs.\ our explicit short-horizon miss regression)---verify HALP's exact training signal in the paper before sharpening wording. \\
\addlinespace[0.15em]
LRB \citep{song2020lrb} &
Relaxed Belady-style learning signals for CDN caching &
CDN cache eviction under learned policy &
Relaxed Belady boundary and training pipeline &
Belady-relaxed learning for CDN eviction; we use finite-horizon LRU rollouts on counterfactual post-eviction caches as regression targets (implementation: repo builder). \\
\addlinespace[0.15em]
Raven \citep{hu2022raven} &
Belady-guided predictive modeling (e.g., reuse-time distributions) &
Eviction guided by learned predictions &
Predictive model serving eviction &
Predictive Belady-guided caching; our method is not framed as learning reuse-time distributions, but as scoring residents with miss-count targets. \\
\addlinespace[0.15em]
Mockingjay \citep{shah2022mockingjay} &
Multiclass / richer reuse modeling to mimic Belady-style behavior &
Cache line replacement using predicted reuse &
Predictor-driven eviction &
Mimicry of Belady MIN via reuse predictions; narrows broad ``learned eviction'' novelty---contrast via explicit counterfactual miss-count supervision. \\
\addlinespace[0.15em]
MUSTACHE \citep{tolomei2022mustache} &
Multi-step-ahead \emph{page request} forecasting to approximate Belady &
Eviction using learned forecaster queries &
Deep time-series forecasting integrated with simulator &
\textbf{Verify nuance:} abstract emphasizes \emph{forecasting future references}; our v1 labels are simulated miss counts after hypothetical evictions (author checklist: \texttt{table6\_related\_work\_uncertainties.md}). \\
\addlinespace[0.15em]
\texttt{evict\_value\_v1} (this work) &
Finite-horizon counterfactual miss counts on candidate victims (LRU continuation in v1 labels) &
Argmin predicted loss over current residents &
Optional guard wrapper; robust baselines evaluated separately &
Positions contribution as \textbf{candidate-level eviction-value learning} with \textbf{decision-aligned local supervision} and multi-trace replay; not a claim that prior methods are incorrect. \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{minipage}
